flashcards.back: A combinatorial optimization problem where items with weights and values must be selected to maximize total value without exceeding a weight capacity.
0/1 Knapsack: items must be taken entirely or not at all. Fractional Knapsack: fractions of items can be taken.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of 0/1 Knapsack using Dynamic Programming?
flashcards.back: O(n*W) where n is number of items and W is capacity.

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of 0/1 Knapsack using DP?
flashcards.back: O(n*W) for table, can be optimized to O(W) using 1D array.

flashcards.front: Write the recurrence relation for 0/1 Knapsack
flashcards.back: K[i][w] = max(K[i-1][w], K[i-1][w-wt[i-1]] + val[i-1]) if wt[i-1] <= w, else K[i-1][w]

flashcards.front: Which algorithm technique is used for 0/1 Knapsack?
flashcards.back: Dynamic Programming - both top-down memoization and bottom-up tabulation.

flashcards.front: Which algorithm technique is used for Fractional Knapsack?
flashcards.back: Greedy Approach - select items by highest value-to-weight ratio first.

flashcards.front: Why is Fractional Knapsack solvable by Greedy but 0/1 is not?
flashcards.back: Greedy fails for 0/1 because taking fractional parts of high-ratio items isn't allowed, leading to suboptimal solutions.
Yes, 0/1 Knapsack is NP-hard, meaning no polynomial-time algorithm exists for arbitrary inputs.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of Fractional Knapsack?
flashcards.back: O(n log n) dominated by sorting by value/weight ratio.

flashcards.front: State when 0/1 Knapsack gives optimal solution using DP
flashcards.back: DP always gives optimal solution for 0/1 Knapsack because it considers all possibilities.

flashcards.front: What is the base case in 0/1 Knapsack DP?
flashcards.back: K[0][w] = 0 (no items) and K[i][0] = 0 (zero capacity) for all i and w.

flashcards.front: Name two real-world applications of Knapsack Problem
flashcards.back: Resource allocation, budget management, cargo loading, inventory management, and portfolio optimization.

flashcards.front: Can Fractional Knapsack be solved using Dynamic Programming?
flashcards.back: Yes, but it's unnecessary since Greedy gives optimal solution in O(n log n), faster than DP's O(n*W).
